note: Sprint 5.15: adds Useful Compute payment proposal v0.1. Converts budget allocations into reviewable payout proposals with worker address mapping and capsule summary. Proposal only: no wallet access, no signing, no broadcast, no consensus. New scripts/trinity/useful_compute_payment_proposal.py reads a Sprint 5.14 reward budget plan plus a worker address map (trinity-worker-address-map/v0.1) and emits a deterministic trinity-useful-compute-payment-proposal/v0.1 with payable_items, unresolved_items, deferred_items, rejected_items and a capsule_summary (template useful_compute_reward_batch_v1, NOT published). Only the primary_workers_share_stocks (70% of allocated) lands in payable_items; replay reserve (20%) and governance reserve (10%) are held back. Workers sharing the same payout_address merge into one payable row. New scripts/trinity/useful_compute_worker_address_map.py helper with create-template + validate subcommands; never generates addresses, never touches a wallet. New schemas/trinity/useful_compute_payment_proposal.schema.json (strict, additionalProperties false, seven const-true safety flags including proposal_only, requires_manual_signing, requires_separate_broadcast). proposal_id is sha16(canonical(pinned_time + source_budget_id + items)) so two runs on the same inputs are byte-identical. Web console gains a Payment Proposal Preview card with payable table, unresolved list, capsule summary and three counters; badge bumped to v0.9. CLI rejects --broadcast, --payout, --send, --wallet, --network, --sign with rc=2. Tests: 62 new across tests/trinity/test_{useful_compute_payment_proposal,useful_compute_payment_proposal_schema,worker_address_map,payment_proposal_web_console}.py; static safety + no-network checks extended to cover the two new scripts. Trinity suite 606/606 PASS with materials-engine-private present. docs/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_PAYMENT_PROPOSAL_V01.md. No consensus, RPC, node, miner, wallet or wire-format change.
